AZ.url_notes: Site is behind Cloudflare protection; direct PDF downloads are blocked by JS challenge. Page itself loads but requires JS to pass Cloudflare challenge for PDF downloads.
AZ.launch_notes: Both retail and online launched September 9, 2021, timed with NFL Week 1
AZ.reporting_lag: Reports released sporadically, sometimes several weeks after reporting month (e.g., Feb 2023 data released May 10)
AZ.archive_depth: From September 2021 (launch) to present; monthly reports available for each month
AZ.requires_js_notes: Cloudflare challenge requires JavaScript to access PDF downloads; standard curl/wget will fail

direct_download_urls.note: URLs are inconsistent in naming convention (dashes, spaces). Cloudflare blocks automated downloads.

AZ.operator_notes: Up to 20 licenses: 10 for tribal nations, 10 for professional sports teams/facilities. Each license can operate up to 2 online sportsbook brands. 14 active online sportsbooks as of late 2025. 4 retail sportsbook locations. Operators are listed by their operator brand name in reports, split by Retail and Mobile columns.
AZ.sport_breakdown_notes: AZ reports do not break down by individual sport. Aggregate statewide handle and revenue only.
AZ.retail_online_notes: Reports separate data into Retail and Mobile columns for each operator. Over 99% of wagers are mobile.

data_fields.name: Operator
data_fields.description: Name of the event wagering operator

data_fields.name: Retail - Gross Event Wagering Receipts (Wagers)
data_fields.description: Total retail wagers placed (handle)

data_fields.name: Mobile - Gross Event Wagering Receipts (Wagers)
data_fields.description: Total mobile wagers placed (handle)

data_fields.name: Retail - Adj Gross Event Wagering Rcpts
data_fields.description: Adjusted gross receipts for retail channel after deductions

data_fields.name: Mobile - Adj Gross Event Wagering Rcpts
data_fields.description: Adjusted gross receipts for mobile channel after deductions

data_fields.name: Retail - Privilege Fee
data_fields.description: 8% tax on positive adjusted gross receipts (retail)

data_fields.name: Mobile - Privilege Fee
data_fields.description: 10% tax on positive adjusted gross receipts (mobile)

AZ.data_field_notes: Privilege fees are net of prior period adjustments. Actual privilege fees are only assessed on positive amounts. Reports for May 2025+ include disclaimer that numbers are self-reported and unaudited.
AZ.ggr_formula: Gross Event Wagering Receipts (Wagers) - Winnings Paid to Participants - Voided Bets - Federal Excise Tax - Promotional Credits/Free Bets (subject to sliding scale cap)
AZ.promo_deduction: Sliding scale that decreases over time from launch (April 15, 2021): Years 1-2: up to 20% of gross wagering receipts; Year 3: up to 15%; Years 4-5: up to 10%; Year 6+: 0% (no deduction allowed). As of 2025, Arizona is in Year 4-5, so the cap is 10%.
AZ.online_tax_rate: 10% (privilege fee on mobile adjusted gross event wagering receipts)
AZ.retail_tax_rate: 8% (privilege fee on retail adjusted gross event wagering receipts)
AZ.tax_notes: Governor proposed HB2732 in 2024 session for a tiered tax rate up to 45% for largest operators, but this has not been enacted as of research date.
AZ.scraping_approach: Cloudflare protection blocks direct curl/wget. Requires headless browser with Playwright/Puppeteer to bypass JS challenge. PDF reports must be parsed with a PDF extraction library (pdfplumber, tabula). Report naming convention is inconsistent across months. Recommend: 1) Use Playwright to navigate to report listing page, 2) Extract PDF links, 3) Download PDFs through browser context, 4) Parse with tabula-py or pdfplumber.
AZ.scraping_time_estimate: 6-8 hours for full implementation including Cloudflare bypass and PDF parsing
AZ.sample_files_notes: All download attempts blocked by Cloudflare JS challenge. Files saved were HTML Cloudflare challenge pages, not actual PDFs.
AZ.notes: Arizona has a unique licensing structure with 10 tribal and 10 professional sports franchise licenses. The promo deduction sliding scale is a key feature - currently in Year 4-5 (10% cap) and will go to 0% in Year 6 (starting ~April 2027). Reports are unaudited self-reported data.
AZ.log: WebFetch to gaming.az.gov returned 403. WebSearch found report URLs and structure. curl downloads returned Cloudflare challenge HTML. Confirmed via web search: PDF format only, monthly frequency, operator-by-operator with retail/mobile split.

IN.launch_notes: Retail launched September 1, 2019. Mobile/online launched October 3, 2019 (BetRivers and DraftKings first).
IN.reporting_lag: Reports typically released around 10th-13th of the following month
IN.archive_depth: Monthly reports from September 2019 (launch) to present. Both PDF and Excel available.
IN.requires_js_notes: Basic download links work without JavaScript. Page uses JS for navigation but downloads are direct.
operators: French Lick Resort - bet365 (bet365.com) and BetRivers (IN.betrivers.com)
Hard Rock Casino Northern Indiana (Gary) - Hard Rock Sportsbook (HardRockSportsbook.com)
Harrah's Hoosier Park (Anderson) - Caesars/WilliamHill (WilliamHill.com) + OTBs in Indianapolis and New Haven
IN.operator_notes: Indiana ties mobile sportsbook licenses to physical casino properties. Each casino can have one or more online sportsbook partners. Reports show the casino name as the primary entity with associated online brands listed in the detail sheet. 13 riverboat casinos and 12 mobile sportsbooks. Some casinos are retail-only with no online partner.
IN.sport_breakdown_notes: Handle by Sport is in Sheet 7 (SW Tax Summary) of the Excel file. Includes both monthly and YTD figures. Note: 'The Handle by Sport numbers are unaudited amounts used for informational purposes and not used in the calculation of taxes.'
IN.retail_online_notes: Sheet 8 (SW Details) breaks down each casino into its online sportsbook brand(s) vs Retail handle and gross receipts separately.

data_fields.name: Casino/Operator Name
data_fields.description: Name of the licensed casino property

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total wagers received including future event wagers

data_fields.name: Taxable AGR
data_fields.description: Adjusted gross revenue after payouts, adjustments, and free play deductions

data_fields.name: Tax
data_fields.description: 9.5% tax on Taxable AGR

data_fields.name: State Wide Handle by Sport
data_fields.description: Breakdown of handle by sport category

data_fields.name: Online Brand Name
data_fields.description: Name and URL of mobile sportsbook operator

data_fields.name: Handle (by brand)
data_fields.description: Handle for each online brand and retail separately

data_fields.name: Gross Receipts (by brand)
data_fields.description: Gross receipts for each online brand and retail separately

data_fields.name: Adjustments
data_fields.description: Prior period adjustments to gross receipts

data_fields.name: Taxable AGR (by brand)
data_fields.description: Final taxable AGR per casino after all adjustments

IN.data_field_notes: Excel file has 8 sheets: 1) Tax Summary (all gaming), 2) YTD Summary, 3-5) Table Games by region, 6) EGD Summary, 7) SW Tax Summary (sports wagering aggregate), 8) SW Details (sports wagering by operator/brand). Sports wagering data is primarily in sheets 7 and 8. Sheet 1 also includes Sports Wagering Tax column alongside Supplemental Tax and Wagering Tax.
IN.ggr_formula: Handle (total wagers) - Payouts on winning wagers - Adjustments. Note: Handle includes wagers received for future events where payout would be in a future month, so win percentage cannot be calculated by dividing revenue by handle for the same period.
IN.promo_deduction: Indiana law allows gaming licensees to deduct adjusted gross revenue attributable to free-play wagering. Total deductions per licensee are capped at $7 million annually.
IN.tax_basis: Adjusted Gross Receipts from sports wagering
IN.tax_notes: Same rate for online and retail. 3.33% of tax receipts allocated to Addiction Services Fund. Harrah's Hoosier Park includes associated OTBs in Indianapolis and New Haven. Horseshoe Indianapolis includes OTB in Clarksville.
IN.scraping_approach: Direct URL pattern for both PDF and XLSX files. No Cloudflare or JS challenges. XLSX is the best format - can be parsed with openpyxl directly. URL pattern is predictable: /igc/files/reports/{YYYY}/{YYYY}-{MM}-Revenue.xlsx. Recommend: 1) Iterate months with predictable URL pattern, 2) Download XLSX files with requests/urllib, 3) Parse sheets 7 and 8 with openpyxl for sports wagering data.
IN.notes: Indiana is one of the easiest states to scrape - predictable URL patterns, XLSX format with structured sheets, no authentication or JS challenges. The Excel file is comprehensive with 8 sheets covering all gaming types. Sports wagering is in sheets 7 (summary with sport breakdown) and 8 (detailed operator/brand breakdown). The report also includes overall casino gaming data (table games, EGDs) which could be useful for cross-referencing.
IN.log: WebFetch returned page structure. curl successfully downloaded PDF and XLSX. openpyxl inspection revealed 8 sheets with detailed sports wagering data in sheets 7 and 8. All operator brands and sport categories confirmed.

MA.launch_notes: Retail launched January 31, 2023 (Category 1 casinos). Online/mobile launched March 10, 2023 (Category 3 operators).
MA.reporting_lag: Reports typically updated around 20th of following month (e.g., February 2026 report updated 3.20.2026)
MA.archive_depth: From January 2023 (retail launch) to present. Revenue Report Archives page has historical data. Monthly summary PDF plus individual operator PDFs.
MA.requires_js_notes: Page uses JavaScript extensively for form functionality, analytics, and dynamic content rendering. However, direct PDF download links work without JS.
MA.operator_notes: Category 1: Licensed casino resort operators (retail). Category 2: Racetrack/simulcast (Plainridge Park Casino is technically Cat 2 for casino but Cat 1 for sports). Category 3: Online/mobile operators. The monthly summary PDF shows all operators in a single page with month-over-month comparison tables going back 12 months. Individual operator PDFs are also available with more detail.
MA.sport_breakdown_notes: Massachusetts reports do not break down by individual sport. Data is aggregate by operator.
MA.retail_online_notes: Summary page clearly separates 'RETAIL LICENSEES (TAXED AT 15%)' and 'ONLINE LICENSEES (TAXED AT 20%)' with separate totals. Category 1 = retail, Category 3 = online.

data_fields.name: Licensee Name
data_fields.description: Name of the sports wagering operator

data_fields.name: Current & Future Wagers Settled
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered on approved sporting events settled in the month (handle)

data_fields.name: Accrual Win by Licensee
data_fields.description: Net win for the licensee (gross revenue before tax deductions)

data_fields.name: Revenue Hold %
data_fields.description: Accrual Win divided by Wagers Settled, expressed as percentage

data_fields.name: Taxable Gaming Revenue
data_fields.description: Amount of accrual win that is taxable after allowable deductions

data_fields.name: Tax Collected
data_fields.description: Tax paid (15% for retail, 20% for online)

MA.data_field_notes: The monthly summary PDF also includes a 'SPORTS WAGERING MONTH OVER MONTH' table showing WAGERS SETTLED, TAXABLE REVENUE, and TAX COLLECTED for each operator going back 12 months. When adjusted gross sports wagering receipts are negative, operators may carry over the negative amount in tax liability to subsequent months.
MA.ggr_definition: Gross Sports Wagering Revenue (GSWR) / Taxable Gaming Revenue
MA.ggr_formula: Total gross receipts from sports wagering minus (total winnings paid to participants + excise taxes paid pursuant to federal law). When GSWR is negative for a month, the operator may carry forward the negative amount for up to one year.
MA.promo_deduction: Promotional play deductions are allowed but details on cap or limits are embedded in individual operator reports. Negative revenue carryover is permitted.
MA.tax_basis: Taxable Gaming Revenue (Gross Sports Wagering Revenue)
MA.tax_notes: Tax revenue allocation: 45% General Fund, 27.5% Gaming Local Aid Fund, 17.5% Workforce Investment Trust Fund, 9% Public Health Trust Fund, 1% Youth Development and Achievement Fund. Casino gaming has separate rates: Cat 1 resorts at 25% GGR, Cat 2 slots-parlor at 49% GGR.
MA.scraping_approach: Direct PDF download URLs follow a predictable pattern. Monthly summary PDF can be downloaded via curl/requests without JS challenges. The PDF is image-based (designed as infographic) so text extraction may require OCR or careful PDF parsing. The month-over-month comparison tables at the end are structured tabular data that should parse well. Recommend: 1) Download monthly summary PDFs using pattern URL, 2) Use pdfplumber or camelot to extract tables, 3) The infographic pages (1-3) may need OCR, but the tabular pages (4-5) should parse directly.
MA.scraping_time_estimate: 4-5 hours for full implementation including PDF parsing of mixed content
MA.notes: Massachusetts has a clean, well-designed monthly report. The summary PDF combines infographic pages (hard to parse) with structured tabular data pages (easy to parse). The month-over-month tables on pages 4-5 are the most valuable - they show WAGERS SETTLED, TAXABLE REVENUE, and TAX COLLECTED for each operator across 12 months in a single table. Individual operator PDFs provide additional detail but the summary PDF is sufficient for most tracking purposes.
MA.log: WebFetch returned page structure with operator list and report format. curl downloaded summary PDF and Year One PDF successfully. PDF inspection confirmed infographic + tabular structure. WebSearch confirmed tax rates, categories, and operator list.

MD.regulatory_body: Maryland Lottery and Gaming Control Agency (MLGCA)
MD.launch_notes: Retail launched December 10, 2021. Mobile/online launched November 23, 2022.
MD.reporting_lag: Reports available within the following month, presented as news releases with linked data
MD.file_format_notes: Monthly data is presented as HTML news release pages on mdgaming.com with linked Excel downloads for detailed data. Also available as HTML tables directly on the revenue reports page.
MD.archive_depth: From December 2021 (retail launch) to present. Older monthly reports available via separate link. Fiscal years run July through June.
MD.requires_js_notes: Page uses standard HTML tables. Google Tag Manager for analytics but data accessible without JS.
operators_retail: Canton Gaming LLC (Canton and Towson locations)
Kathe P. Hospitality-Michael's on the South River
MD.operator_notes: 13 retail sportsbook locations and 12 mobile platforms as of late 2025. bet365 launched via partnership with licensee Riverboat on the Potomac in August 2025. Canton Gaming opened new location in Pikesville October 2025. Top operators by handle: Live! Casino (FanDuel), DraftKings, BetMGM, Maryland Stadium Sub (Fanatics), Riverboat on the Potomac (bet365).
MD.sport_breakdown_notes: Statewide handle and hold by sport are included in monthly reports. Parlay is tracked separately. 'Other' covers sports not in the main categories.
MD.retail_online_notes: Reports clearly separate retail and mobile handle, prizes, and hold. Retail handle is a small fraction of total (~1.8% in October 2025).

data_fields.name: Sportsbook/Operator Name
data_fields.description: Name of the licensed sportsbook (retail or mobile)

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered

data_fields.name: Prizes Paid
data_fields.description: Total winnings paid to bettors

data_fields.name: Hold Percentage
data_fields.description: Percentage retained by sportsbook (handle - prizes) / handle

data_fields.name: Promotional Play
data_fields.description: Value of promotional credits and free bets

data_fields.name: Taxable Win
data_fields.description: Hold minus promotional play and other deductions

data_fields.name: State Contribution
data_fields.description: Tax payment to the state

data_fields.name: Handle by Sport
data_fields.description: Statewide breakdown of handle by sport category

data_fields.name: Hold by Sport
data_fields.description: Statewide hold percentage by sport category

MD.data_field_notes: Monthly reports also include year-over-year comparisons, fiscal year summaries, and cumulative contribution tracking from inception (December 2021). Fund allocation details (Blueprint for Maryland's Future Fund, General Fund, Problem Gambling Fund) are included.
MD.ggr_formula: Handle - Prizes Paid - Promotional Play = Taxable Win. Hold = Handle - Prizes Paid. Taxable Win = Hold - Promotional Wagers - Other Deductions.
MD.promo_deduction: Promotional play is deductible from hold to calculate taxable win. No specific published cap on promotional deductions found in research. Public comment period on promotional play regulations was active in 2025.
MD.tax_notes: Tax rate for mobile operators increased from 15% to 20% on June 1, 2025 (HB 352 signed by Gov. Wes Moore). Retail remains 15%. Mobile operators contribute 15% to Blueprint for Maryland's Future Fund and 5% to General Fund. FY2025 generated nearly $89M in sports betting tax revenue, up 47.4% from FY2024. Fiscal years run July-June.
MD.scraping_approach: Monthly reports are published as news release pages with HTML tables and linked Excel downloads. The revenue reports landing page has HTML tables with summary data. Recommend: 1) Scrape the revenue reports page for summary data, 2) Follow individual monthly report links for detailed operator data, 3) Download Excel files when available for structured data. News release URLs are not fully predictable (based on dollar amounts in title).
MD.sample_files_notes: Did not attempt Excel download as URL patterns for Excel files were not identified in research. News release HTML pages were analyzed via WebFetch.
MD.notes: Maryland has a rich data set including operator breakdown, sport breakdown, promotional play tracking, and retail/mobile split. The tax rate increase in June 2025 (15% to 20% for mobile) is a significant change to track. Fiscal year runs July-June. Reports include cumulative inception-to-date totals. The sport breakdown is one of the most detailed among Tier 2 states.
MD.log: WebFetch returned page structure with summary data. WebSearch confirmed operators, tax rates, sport categories. Tax rate change from 15% to 20% for mobile confirmed effective June 1, 2025.

VA.url_notes: The originally provided URL (https://www.valottery.com/winnersnews/latestwinners) was WRONG - it points to lottery winners, not sports betting revenue. The correct URL for sports betting revenue info is the one listed here. However, this page only explains the tax methodology, not actual data.
VA.actual_report_source: Virginia Lottery submits monthly reports to the Governor and legislature. Reports are published as PDFs through the Virginia Legislative Documents system (rga.lis.virginia.gov).
VA.launch_notes: FanDuel launched first on January 21, 2021. DraftKings followed January 24, 2021. BetMGM and BetRivers launched January 27, 2021. Virginia is online-only initially; retail sportsbooks opened later at land-based casinos.
VA.reporting_lag: Reports typically released about 4 weeks after month end. Published as formal letters to the Governor.
VA.archive_depth: Monthly reports from January 2021 (launch) to present, published through Virginia legislative document system
VA.requires_js_notes: PDFs are direct downloads from rga.lis.virginia.gov. No JS required.

direct_download_urls.note: RD numbers are not sequential by month - they are legislative document IDs. Need to find the mapping or scrape the listing page.

VA.operator_notes: Virginia authorizes up to 12 online operator permits. 11 mobile operators and 3 land-based casinos active as of November 2025 (Danville Casino, Rivers Casino Portsmouth, Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Bristol). IMPORTANT: Virginia reports do NOT break down by individual operator. Reports provide only statewide aggregate data. Operator names are known from licensing but not from revenue reports.
VA.sport_breakdown_notes: Virginia reports do not break down by sport. Only aggregate statewide data.
VA.retail_online_notes: Virginia reports do not separate retail vs online. Only aggregate statewide totals. The November 2025 report mentions '11 mobile sports betting operators and three land-based casinos' but does not split the data.

data_fields.name: Total wagers
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered by eligible players (handle)

data_fields.name: Winnings
data_fields.description: Cash winnings paid to players

data_fields.name: Noncash Winnings (eligible promotions)
data_fields.description: Value of eligible noncash promotional winnings (only in first 12 months of operator's operation)

data_fields.name: Void and Cancelled wagers
data_fields.description: Wagers that were voided or cancelled

data_fields.name: Federal excise taxes
data_fields.description: Federal excise taxes paid on wagering activity

data_fields.name: Adjustments
data_fields.description: Certain allowable adjustments

data_fields.name: Total, Virginia Adjusted Gross Revenues
data_fields.description: Final AGR after all deductions

data_fields.name: General Fund
data_fields.description: 97.5% of total taxes allocated to state general fund

data_fields.name: Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund
data_fields.description: 2.5% of total taxes allocated to problem gambling fund

data_fields.name: Total Taxes
data_fields.description: 15% of AGR

VA.data_field_notes: Report includes both 'Monthly Activity' and 'FY Total' (fiscal year to date) columns. Report is formatted as a formal letter from the Virginia Lottery Executive Director to the Governor. Data tables are embedded in the letter format. Virginia Code section 58.1-4031 mandates these reports.
VA.ggr_formula: Total Wagers - Cash Winnings - Eligible Noncash Winnings (first 12 months only) - Voided/Cancelled Wagers - Federal Excise Taxes - Certain Allowable Adjustments = AGR. Negative AGR may be carried forward to the following month for up to one year.
VA.promo_deduction: Eligible noncash winnings during an operator's first twelve months of operation can be deducted. After the first 12 months, promotional deductions are not allowed per the 2025 Appropriation Act (Chapter 725). This is a significant restriction compared to other states.
VA.tax_notes: Tax distribution: 97.5% to General Fund, 2.5% to Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund. Virginia Code sections 58.1-4030 through 58.1-4038 govern sports betting taxation. Negative AGR carryforward allowed for up to one year.
VA.scraping_approach: Reports are published through Virginia's legislative document system (rga.lis.virginia.gov) as PDF letters. The document IDs (RD numbers) are not sequential by month, making URL construction unpredictable. Recommend: 1) Scrape the legislative document listing page to find report URLs, or 2) Use WebFetch/search to discover each month's RD number. PDFs are formal letters with embedded tables - need PDF parsing to extract tabular data. Data is aggregate only (no operator or sport breakdown), so each PDF yields just a few numbers.
VA.scraping_time_estimate: 5-7 hours (URL discovery is the main challenge)
VA.notes: IMPORTANT: The URL provided in the task (valottery.com/winnersnews/latestwinners) was incorrect - it's the lottery winners page, not sports betting revenue. The actual revenue explanation is at valottery.com/aboutus/casinosandsportsbetting/sportsbettingrevenue, but this page only describes methodology, not data. Actual reports are published through the Virginia legislative document system. Virginia provides the LEAST granular data among Tier 2 states: no operator breakdown, no sport breakdown, no retail/online split. Only aggregate statewide AGR data is published. Promotional deductions are very restrictive - only allowed during an operator's first 12 months.
VA.log: WebFetch to provided URL confirmed it was the wrong page (lottery winners). WebSearch found correct URL and report location. Downloaded November 2025 report PDF from rga.lis.virginia.gov. PDF confirmed formal letter format with aggregate-only data tables.

CO.regulatory_body: Colorado Division of Gaming, Department of Revenue - Specialized Business Group (SBG)
CO.url_notes: URL returns 403 via WebFetch and curl. The site blocks automated access. Page is accessible via browser.
CO.launch_notes: Both retail and mobile sports betting launched May 1, 2020, less than 6 months after voters approved Proposition DD in November 2019.
CO.reporting_lag: Reports typically released 4-5 weeks after end of reporting month. No fixed publication date.
CO.file_format_notes: Monthly HB 1292 Sports Betting Proceeds Reports are published as PDFs. The Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission (CLGCC) meeting materials also contain sports betting data in larger PDFs.
CO.archive_depth: From May 2020 (launch) to present. Monthly reports available by year.
CO.requires_js_notes: sbg.colorado.gov blocks automated access (403). Requires browser to access. CLGCC meeting material PDFs are also served from the same domain with same restrictions.

direct_download_urls.note: All direct download attempts returned HTML (403/redirect). URLs require browser session.

CO.operator_notes: Colorado has an open licensing model allowing up to 30 online sportsbooks. 14-16 active online sportsbooks as of mid-2025. IMPORTANT: Colorado does NOT publish individual operator performance data in its public reports. Only aggregate statewide data is published. Operator names are known from licensing but not from revenue reports.
CO.sport_breakdown_notes: Colorado reports break down handle by sport category. Notably, table tennis was the 3rd largest sport by handle in 2025 at $32M+, ahead of NCAA football.
CO.retail_online_notes: Colorado does NOT separate retail vs online in its public revenue reports. Only aggregate data is published.

data_fields.name: Total Handle
data_fields.description: Total wagers placed statewide

data_fields.name: Net Sports Betting Proceeds (NSBP)
data_fields.description: Taxable revenue (GGR minus promotional deductions and federal excise tax)

data_fields.name: Taxes Collected
data_fields.description: 10% tax on net sports betting proceeds

data_fields.name: Handle by Sport
data_fields.description: Breakdown of total handle by sport category

CO.data_field_notes: Colorado provides less granular data than many other states. No operator breakdown, no retail/online split. Sport breakdown is available. Monthly and fiscal year-to-date figures are provided.
CO.ggr_formula: Gross Gaming Revenue (Handle - Payouts) - Promotional Credits/Free Bets (subject to sunset schedule) - Federal Excise Tax (0.25% of handle) = Net Sports Betting Proceeds
CO.promo_deduction: Promotional deductions undergoing major legislative change via HB25-1311: July 1 - Dec 31, 2025: max 2% of total free bets per month deductible; Jan 1 - Jun 30, 2026: max 1.75% deductible; July 1, 2026+: 0% - promotional deductions eliminated entirely. This represents a full sunset of promo deductions.
CO.tax_notes: Flat 10% tax rate approved by voters in Proposition DD (2019). Revenue allocated to water conservation, responsible gaming programs, and other state priorities. FY2025 tax revenue exceeded $37M. January 2026 set a record with $5.02M in tax revenue.
CO.scraping_approach: sbg.colorado.gov blocks automated access. Requires headless browser (Playwright/Puppeteer) to navigate to monthly reports page. PDFs are served from the same domain with same restrictions. Recommend: 1) Use Playwright to open the monthly reports page, 2) Locate and click PDF links, 3) Download PDFs through browser context, 4) Parse PDFs for aggregate data. Since there's no operator breakdown, the data extracted per month is relatively simple (handle, NSBP, taxes, sport breakdown).
CO.scraping_time_estimate: 5-7 hours (403 bypass with browser automation is the main challenge)
CO.sample_files_notes: All download attempts returned HTML 403 pages instead of actual PDFs. sbg.colorado.gov blocks curl/wget entirely.
CO.notes: Colorado is one of the less transparent states for public data. No operator breakdown and no retail/online split makes it less useful for competitive analysis. The sport breakdown is a unique feature. The promo deduction sunset (HB25-1311) is a major policy change - deductions go to 0% by July 2026. The 10% tax rate is one of the lowest among legal sports betting states. Table tennis appearing as a top sport category is unusual and may reflect offshore/synthetic betting markets.
CO.log: WebFetch returned 403 for sbg.colorado.gov. All curl attempts returned HTML. WebSearch confirmed report structure, sport categories, tax rates, and promo deduction sunset schedule. No sample files could be downloaded.
